segments.text: BETH: Oh good morning. You must be James.
segments.translation: 贝丝：早上好。你一定是詹姆斯。

segments.text: I'm Beth Cartwright - please call me Beth.
segments.translation: 我是贝丝 卡特莱特——请叫我贝丝。

segments.text: JAMES: Thank you.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：谢谢。

segments.text: BETH: Now as this is your first tutorial since you started on the Scandinavian Studies course, I'd like to find out something about you.
segments.translation: 贝丝：因为这是你选择斯堪的纳维亚研究课后的第一次辅导课，我想了解一下你的情况。

segments.text: Why did you decide to take this course?
segments.translation: 你为什么选择这门课程呢？

segments.text: JAMES: Well, my mother is Danish, and although we always lived in England, she used to talk about her home a lot, and that made me want to visit Denmark.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：我的母亲是丹麦人，尽管我们都生活在英格兰。她经常谈论她的家乡，这就使得我想去丹麦看一看。

segments.text: We hardly ever did, though - my mother usually went on her own.
segments.translation: 虽然我们几乎没办法一起去——但是我母亲通常独自去。

segments.text: But whenever her relations or friends were in England they always came to see us.
segments.translation: 但是无论何时，他的亲戚朋友到英格兰来，他们都会来看看我们。

segments.text: BETH: I see. So I assume you already speak Danish, one of the languages you'll be studying.
segments.translation: 贝丝：我明白了。所以我猜你已经会说丹麦语了，你正在学习的语言之一。

segments.text: JAMES: I can get by when I talk to people, though I'm not terribly accurate.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：虽然我讲的不是很准确，但是跟人们谈话的时候还是可以理解的。

segments.text: BETH: Now you probably know that you'll spend the third year of the course abroad.
segments.translation: 贝丝：现在或许你知道你将在第三年出国学习。

segments.text: Have you had any thoughts about that?
segments.translation: 你有什么想法吗？

segments.text: JAMES: I'm really looking forward to it.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：我真的已经很期待了。

segments.text: And although Denmark seems the obvious place to go, because of my family connections, I'd love to spend the time in Iceland.
segments.translation: 尽管丹麦很明显是我应该去的地方，因为我家庭的原因，但是我也想去冰岛度假。

segments.text: BETH: Oh, I'm sure it can be arranged.
segments.translation: 贝丝：我保证，会安排的。

segments.text: Do you have any plans for when you graduate?
segments.translation: 你有毕业后有什么计划吗？

segments.text: A lot of students go on to take a master's degree.
segments.translation: 很多学生继续攻读硕士学位。

segments.text: JAMES: I think the four years of the undergraduate course will be enough for me.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：我想四年的本科学业对我来说足够了。

segments.text: I'm interested in journalism and I quite like the idea of moving to Scandinavia and writing for magazines.
segments.translation: 我对新闻学感兴趣，我非常想搬到斯堪的纳维亚，为杂志社写文章。

segments.text: I'd find that more creative than translating, which I suppose most graduates do.
segments.translation: 我认为那会比翻译更有创造力，我猜这也是很多毕业生想做的。

segments.text: BETH: OK. Now how are you finding the courses you're taking this term, James?
segments.translation: 贝丝：好的。你认为这学期你的课程怎么样，詹姆斯？

segments.text: JAMES: Well, I'm really enjoying the one on Swedish cinema.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：嗯，我非常喜欢瑞典电影学的课程。

segments.text: BETH: That'll continue next term, but the one on Scandinavian literature that's running at the moment will be replaced by more specialised courses.
segments.translation: 贝丝： 那个课程下学期也会继续，但是现在开设的斯堪的纳维亚文学将会被更多的专业课代替。

segments.text: Oh, and by the way, if you're interested in watching Danish television programmes - there's going to be a course on that the term after next.
segments.translation: 噢，顺便说一下，如果你喜欢看丹麦电视节目——在下下学期会有一门关于它的课程。

segments.text: JAMES: That sounds good.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：听起来不错。

segments.text: BETH: Have you started thinking about the literature paper that you have to write in the next few weeks?
segments.translation: 贝丝：你开始考虑几周以后要写的文学论文了吗？

segments.text: JAMES: Yes, my first choice would be to do something on the Icelandic sagas.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：是的，我的首选是写冰岛传说。

segments.text: BETH: Hmm. The trouble with that is that a lot of people choose that topic, and it can be difficult to get hold of the books you'll need.
segments.translation: 贝丝：嗯。问题是很多人都选了那个主题，所以要拿到你需要的书籍会比较困难。

segments.text: Why not leave that for another time?
segments.translation: 为什么不留给下次尝试呢？

segments.text: JAMES: Right.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：好的。

segments.text: BETH: You might find modern novels or 19th century playwrights interesting.
segments.translation: 贝丝：你会发现现代小说或者19世纪剧作家的主题非常有意思。

segments.text: JAMES: I've read or seen several plays in translation, so that would be a good idea.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：我已经读过或看过一些翻译的戏剧，所以这是个好主意。

segments.text: BETH: Fine. I'll put you down for that topic.
segments.translation: 贝丝：好的。我会把你这个话题记下来的。

segments.text: JAMES: Right. So what would you advise me to aim at in the paper?
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：好的。所以您建议我论文的主旨是什么呢？

segments.text: BETH: First I suggest you avoid taking one writer and going into a great deal of detail.
segments.translation: 贝丝：首先，我建议你避免只谈论一位作者然后给出大量细节。

segments.text: That approach certainly has its place, but I think you first need to get an understanding of the literature in the context of the society in which it was produced - who it was written for, how it was published, and so on.
segments.translation: 那种方法当然也会有他的优点，但是我认为首先你需要理解文学作品在创作时的社会环境——作品是写给谁的，是如何出版的等等。

segments.text: I also think that's more fruitful than placing it within the history of the genre.
segments.translation: 此外，我认为这会比把作品放到文学流派的发展历史中讨论更富有成效。

segments.text: JAMES: OK, that sounds reasonable.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：好的，听起来很有道理。

segments.text: JAMES: Could I ask for some advice about writing the paper I'm working on about the Vikings?
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：您可以给我一点建议吗，关于我正在写的论文，有关维京人的。

segments.text: I have to do that this week, and I'm a bit stuck.
segments.translation: 我必须要在这周内完成，我遇到点困难。

segments.text: BETH: Of course. Have you decided yet what to write about?
segments.translation: 贝丝：当然。你已经决定好要写什么了吗？

segments.text: JAMES: No, I haven't.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：不，没有。

segments.text: There's so much that seems interesting - Viking settlement in other countries, trade, mythology...
segments.translation: 有太多有感兴趣的话题了——维京人定居其他国家，贸易，神话……

segments.text: BETH: Well, what I suggest is that you read an assignment a student wrote last year, which is kept in the library.
segments.translation: 贝丝：好的，我的建议是你读一篇去年学生写的作业，可以在图书馆找到。

segments.text: It's short and well focused, and I'm sure you'll find it helpful.
segments.translation: 文章简洁并且要点集中，我保证你会觉得很有帮助。

segments.text: I'll give you the details in a moment.
segments.translation: 我一会儿给你一些细节。

segments.text: Textbooks usually cover so many topics, it can be very difficult to choose just one.
segments.translation: 课本上通常会包含很多话题，从中选择一个会非常困难。

segments.text: JAMES: OK. I've got a DVD of the film about the Vikings that came out earlier this year.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：好的。我有一张光盘，是关于维京人的电影，是今年早期出版的。

segments.text: Should I watch that again?
segments.translation: 我应该再看一遍吗？

segments.text: BETH: If it's the one I am thinking of, hmm, I'd ignore it - it's more fantasy than reality.
segments.translation: 贝丝：如果是我想的那种，额，就不用看了——那个比事实更虚幻一些。

segments.text: But I've got a recording of a documentary that you should watch.
segments.translation: 但是我有一套纪录片，你应该看一下。

segments.text: It makes some interesting and provocative points, which I think will help you to focus your topic.
segments.translation: 里面有一些有趣的，和激进的观点，我相信会帮助你聚焦你的话题。

segments.text: JAMES: Right.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：好的。

segments.text: JAMES: So then should I work out an outline?
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：所以我应该列提纲吗？

segments.text: BETH: Yes. Just headings for different sections, at this stage.
segments.translation: 贝丝：是的。只是把每个部分的标题列一下就行了，就到这个程度就可以。

segments.text: And then you should start looking for suitable articles and books to draw on, and take notes which you organise according to those headings.
segments.translation: 然后你应该开始寻找合适的文章和书籍借鉴参考，记笔记。根据那些标题组织内容。

segments.text: JAMES: I see.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：我明白了。

segments.text: BETH: Then put short phrases and sentences as bullet points under each heading.
segments.translation: 贝丝：然后在每个标题下列出短语和句子作为要点。

segments.text: Make sure that this skeleton makes sense and flows properly, before writing up the paper in full.
segments.translation: 确保提纲有意义并且行文流畅，接下来在完整的写全文。

segments.text: JAMES: OK. Thanks, that's very helpful.
segments.translation: 詹姆斯：好的。谢谢，非常有帮助。
